Brandon Severin

Coda by Conductor Quantum

Coda by Conductor Quantum

Maker

📌

👋 Hi Product Hunters!  
  
I’m Brandon, co-founder of Conductor Quantum. Today we’re launching Coda, a natural language interface for quantum computers.  
  
Coda lets you design, understand, and run quantum programs in natural language, without writing low-level quantum code. We built it because quantum computing is powerful, but still far too hard to actually use.

Quantum computers offer a new computing paradigm for simulating the world at the atomic level. This opens up new possibilities in chemistry, materials science, and drug discovery that push beyond what the largest classical supercomputers can handle.

results.title: Upstash Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
5.0

Based on 31 reviews

Review Upstash?

Reviewers describe Upstash as a practical way to avoid running Kafka or Redis infrastructure yourself, with users praising solid performance, speed, scalability, and smooth Redis support. The feedback is sparse but consistently positive, with no reported downsides. Founder feedback adds concrete use cases: the makers of Notra say Upstash Box saved boilerplate, the team behind ZeroGPU rely on it for low-latency caching, and the makers of Coda by Conductor Quantum use it for orchestration and state.

Pull Sense

used Upstash to build Pull Sense [...] 2 views

Pull Sense

used Upstash to build Pull Sense

•15 reviews

Upstash provides serverless Redis and Kafka with pay-per-request pricing, making it a perfect fit for modern, event-driven applications. Unlike traditional Redis providers that require constant uptime billing, Upstash’s serverless model ensures cost efficiency. It integrates seamlessly with Vercel, Railway, and edge functions, making it an ideal choice for PullSense to handle caching and real-time workloads without worrying about infrastructure management.

Pi, your personal AI

## Pi, your personal AI

## A kind and supportive companion that’s on your side

4.8•10 reviews•

268 followers

## A kind and supportive companion that’s on your side

4.8•10 reviews•

268 followers

Visit website

A new class of AI, Pi is designed to offer conversations, friendly advice, and concise information in a natural, flowing style. Pi is very knowledgeable, always curious and patient, and happy to help you in every way it can. Tell Pi about a topic you're interested in, from quantum physics to movie trivia. Ask for advice about the big and small decisions in your life. Get motivating tips for how to learn to play an instrument, stay healthy, or pick up a new hobby.

 Overview
 Launches2
 Reviews10
 Alternatives
 Team [...] Based on 10 reviews

Review Pi, your personal AI?

Reviewers mostly praise Pi for warm, human-like conversation, saying it feels supportive, smooth, and easy to confide in during hard times. Several highlight its natural-sounding voice, fast responses, and seamless use across phone and web, with some calling it better at companionship than work or learning tasks. The main complaints are narrower: weaker performance in languages other than English, including an English accent in voice, stricter guardrails that can cut off light chats, and a few account syncing or device compatibility limits.

Image 55: Didar

Didar

•1 review

I recently tried nFactorial AI, and I was impressed. It features AI-powered versions of legendary minds who deliver focused, 5-minute presentations on their areas of expertise. It’s a fantastic tool for self-education and personal growth — you can quickly grasp key insights from some of the world’s best thinkers. What I especially liked is that after the presentation, you can continue the conversation by chatting with the AI tutor about the topic or even generate a brand-new presentation on demand. It’s engaging, efficient, and makes high-quality learning accessible anytime. Highly recommended for anyone looking to learn faster and smarter.

Image 54: saulo assis

saulo assis

•2 reviews

I found the concept of avatars really cool, I tested it and was impressed. I’d like to highlight the presentation during the video conference: I was truly surprised by the consistency and structure of the narrative, including the synchronization with the slides. After that, I also tested the Q&A chat with the avatar, and once again the prompt really impressed me with the depth and specialization of the topic. I see this as an excellent prototype for implementation in specialist blogs. Congratulations to the team for the great work!
